items.title: Kyndryl (NYSE:KD) Misses Q2 Sales Targets, Stock Drops 13%
items.description: IT infrastructure services provider Kyndryl (NYSE:KD) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2025, with sales flat year on year at $3.74 billion. Next quarter’s revenue guidance of $3.81 billion underwhelmed, coming in 1.5% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.37 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

items.title: Kyndryl's first-quarter profit soars as renegotiated contracts aid margins
items.description: The company, formerly IBM's infrastructure services business, has been restructuring multiple no-margin contracts it inherited from the Big Blue to generate higher profits, but at the cost of revenue growth.  Kyndryl's net income soared to $56 million for the June quarter from a profit of $11 million in the year-ago-period, while revenue was flat at $3.74 billion.  Businesses have prioritized spending on essential software amid macroeconomic uncertainty spurred by U.S. President Donald Trump's ongoing global trade negotiations.

items.title: Don't Trust Cloud Utilization, Says Todd Smith of TAHO
items.description: VENICE, FL / ACCESS Newswire / August 4, 2025 / Cloud computing powers everything from social media feeds to advanced AI tools. But as companies scale up their cloud infrastructure, their costs are spiraling and many don't know why. Veteran tech leader ...

items.title: Will AMD Stock Climb on Strong Data Center Revenues in Q2 Earnings?
items.description: Advanced Micro Devices' second-quarter 2025 earnings are likely to benefit from strong client and data center growth amid weakness in the Embedded segment.

items.title: SNIA Announces Storage.AI
items.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif., August 04, 2025--SNIA announced Storage.AI™, an open standards project for efficient data services related to AI workloads.
